build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: An alchemy map shows a 2D space with ingredient zones. The player navigates a cursor from the center outward, passing through zones that add properties to the brew. The path taken determines the potion type. Score 0 if no alchemy map navigation exists. Score 0.5 at most if a map exists but path does not affect potion outcome. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Reaching a recipe zone on the alchemy map and bottling creates a specific potion type (healing, fire resistance, invisibility, etc.). At least 5 distinct potion types are discoverable. Score 0 if no recipe/bottling system exists. Score 0.5 at most if potions can be made but fewer than 4 types exist. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Customers arrive with orders showing desired potion types via icons. Fulfilling an order earns gold and reputation. Failing or ignoring orders loses reputation. Score 0 if no customer order system exists. Score 0.5 at most if customers exist but orders have no consequence. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

A reputation system tracks shop standing. High reputation unlocks rarer ingredients and harder customers. Losing all reputation ends the run. Reputation is visible and changes with each fulfilled or failed order. Score 0 if no reputation system exists. Score 0.5 at most if reputation exists but has no gameplay consequence. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Gold earned from sales buys map reveals (uncovering hidden ingredient zones), better tools (faster cursor, wider paths), and ingredient restocks. The economy creates meaningful spending decisions. Score 0 if no shop or spending exists. Score 0.5 at most if gold exists but has nothing meaningful to spend on. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The alchemy map is partially hidden and revealed through exploration or purchases. At least 5 distinct ingredient zones exist with different visual identities and effects on the brew. Score 0 if the map is fully visible from the start or has fewer than 4 zones. Score 0.5 at most if zones exist but are not visually distinct. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Customer variety increases over days — early customers want simple potions, later customers want rare or complex recipes. At least 3 tiers of customer difficulty exist. Score 0 if all customers want the same thing. Score 0.5 at most if variety exists but difficulty does not escalate. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Recipe discovery is experimental — the player must explore the alchemy map to find where recipe zones are located. A recipe book records discovered recipes for future reference. Score 0 if all recipes are known from the start. Score 0.5 at most if discovery exists but no recipe book records findings. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Each run generates a new alchemy map layout — ingredient zones are in different positions, requiring re-discovery of recipe paths. No two runs have identical maps. Score 0 if the map is always the same. Score 0.5 at most if some variation exists but recipe locations are predictable. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A run-end evaluation shows days survived, reputation earned, gold total, recipes discovered, and orders fulfilled. The evaluation reflects actual performance. Score 0 if no results exist. Score 0.5 at most if only basic info is shown. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A styled title screen shows bubbling cauldrons and potion bottles with the game name. Score 0 if the game starts directly in gameplay or uses raw default UI. Score 0.5 at most if a title exists but is sparse. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The game flow connects title -> shop day -> customer orders -> alchemy brewing -> delivery -> next day without restarting. Score 0 if transitions are missing. Score 0.5 at most if flow exists but is broken. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

During brewing, the alchemy map shows the cursor position, explored/unexplored areas, ingredient zones, and recipe targets. Customer orders and reputation are visible. Score 0 if no map visualization exists. Score 0.5 at most if the map is unreadable. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Readability at 1280x720 is strong: ingredient zones, recipe targets, cursor path, customers, and potion types are all clearly distinguishable. Score 0 if elements blend together. Score 0.5 at most if visuals are dominated by raw shapes. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The art direction is coherent across title, alchemy map, shop, customers, and UI — a consistent medieval alchemy aesthetic with warm palette. Score 0 if mostly default UI or mismatched placeholders dominate. Score 0.5 at most if visuals are sparse or generic. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

The alchemy map uses illustrated ingredient art, potion bottles have distinct visual designs, and customers have character sprites — not plain shapes or text. Score 0 if map elements are colored dots. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI chrome: reputation bar, gold counter, recipe book, customer panels, and shop interface use styled themed widgets — not raw unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0 if primary UI elements are obviously unstyled. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation and feedback: the cursor leaves a trail on the alchemy map, bottling has a bubbling animation, customers react visually to fulfilled/failed orders, and reputation changes have visual flair. Score 0 if all state changes snap silently. Score 0.5 at most if animation is minimal. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
